name: Hot and Spicy Sloppy Joes
recipeIngredient: 6 oz can hot-style tomato juice or vegetable cocktail
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook ground beef, onion, and garlic in a large skillet until the meat is brown and the onions are tender. Drain off excess fat.
2. In a 3 1/2 or 4 quart crockery cooker, combine tomato juice (or vegetable cocktail), catsup, water, brown sugar, chopped jalapeno peppers (if using), prepared mustard, chili powder, and Worcestershire sauce. Stir in the meat mixture. Cover and cook on low heat setting for 10-12 hours or high heat setting for 3-5 hours.
3. Toast hamburger buns. Spoon the meat mixture onto the buns and sprinkle with shredded cheese. Serve.
For a 5 or 6 quart crockery cooker, double all ingredients and follow the same steps. This will make approximately 16 servings.
For a 1 quart crockery cooker, omit water from the list of ingredients. Use 1 pound ground beef, 1 medium onion, 1 small clove garlic, one 6 oz can hot-style tomato juice, 1/4 cup catsup, 2 tablespoons brown sugar, 2 tablespoons chopped canned jalapeno peppers (if using), 1 tablespoon prepared mustard, 1 teaspoon chili powder, and 1/2 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce. Cook for 6-10 hours. This will serve approximately 4-5 people.
